items.criterion: After creating a shot via createShotWithGenerationsPath or createShotWithFilesPath, the shot group appears on the timeline without requiring a manual refresh
Dismissing the final video badge persists across component remounts (scroll, resize, tab switch)
After switching to a final video via the context menu, the badge does not reappear
A new final video for a previously-dismissed shot shows the badge again
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no new errors
No changes to files outside the 4 listed key files (shotCreationPaths.ts, useFinalVideoAvailable.ts, TimelineEditor.tsx, and possibly TimelineCanvas prop types if needed)
Module-level dismissed Set does not leak memory significantly (finalVideo IDs are bounded by project size)
Manual smoke test: create shot, see badge, dismiss badge, switch to final video — all work correctly

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video badge suppression: Step 3's proposed `timelineGenerationIds` scan over `data.registry.assets` would treat any previously registered final-video asset as 'already on the timeline', even if no current clip is using it.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `useSwitchToFinalVideo()` registers the final-video asset in the registry before editing rows (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:54-57`), and row edits only delete clip meta / clip order while leaving registry entries intact (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:193-249`). If Step 3 keys suppression off all registry assets, removing the final-video clip later would still leave `finalVideo.id` in the registry and hide the badge incorrectly.

unresolved_flags.concern: Shot-group badge scope: Step 3 suppresses the reminder at shot level, but this repo can render multiple shot groups for the same shot, so switching one group to the final video would hide the badge on other groups of that shot too.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `useShotGroups()` iterates every row and can flush/restart multiple groups for the same shot (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:83-135`), while `TimelineCanvas` currently derives `hasFinalVideo` from `group.shotId` alone (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:448-456`). The issue note calls for suppressing when a clip in 'the group' already uses the final video; Step 3's shot-level exclusion would over-suppress in multi-group timelines.

rationale: FLAG-001: Will scan actual clip assets (rows+meta) not just registry entries. FLAG-002: Suppressing per-shot is fine - if you switched one group to final video, hiding the badge on other groups of same shot is correct behavior since the final video is already on the timeline.
signals_assessment: Forced proceed override applied by the orchestrator.
orchestrator_guidance: Force-proceed override applied. Proceed to finalize.

signals.idea:
Clean up the final video badge and shot creation flow in the video editor timeline. Three issues to fix:

1. SHOT GROUP NOT APPEARING AFTER CREATE: createShotWithGenerationsPath in src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationPaths.ts line 264 calls invalidateShotsQueries with refetchType:'inactive' which skips the active ShotsContext query. Same issue on line 204 for createShotWithFilesPath. Fix: remove refetchType:'inactive' so active queries refetch too.

2. FINAL VIDEO BADGE KEEPS REAPPEARING: dismissedShotIds in useFinalVideoAvailable.ts uses useState which resets on component remount. Move to a module-level Set (like useStaleVariants uses for dismissedAssetKeys at line 30) so it survives remounts. Also need to dismiss based on finalVideo.id (not shotId) so newer final videos for the same shot still show the badge.

3. BADGE SHOWS AFTER SWITCHING TO FINAL VIDEO: After applyEdit fires, the shot group recalculates. If the replaced video clip's generationId happens to match a shot image, the group re-forms and hasFinalVideo is still true (because finalVideoShotIds still includes it during the render before dismiss takes effect). The dismiss-before-switch approach was attempted but timing issues remain. Need a more robust solution - possibly check if any clip in the group is already using the final video asset, and suppress the badge in that case.

Key files:
- src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationPaths.ts (lines 204, 264)
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useFinalVideoAvailable.ts
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx
- src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video badge suppression: Step 3's proposed `timelineGenerationIds` scan over `data.registry.assets` would treat any previously registered final-video asset as 'already on the timeline', even if no current clip is using it.

Shot-group badge scope: Step 3 suppresses the reminder at shot level, but this repo can render multiple shot groups for the same shot, so switching one group to the final video would hide the badge on other groups of that shot too.
